text: When building websites, security is extremely important. First, always use HTTPS to encrypt data between the user's browser and the server. This prevents hackers from reading sensitive information like passwords. Second, implement strong authentication systems, such as requiring complex passwords and two-factor verification. Third, validate all user input on the server side. Never trust data directly from users, as it may contain malicious code that can lead to SQL injection or cross-site scripting attacks. These are common vulnerabilities that can expose or destroy your database. Finally, keep your software updated to patch known security flaws. By following these practices, you can protect both your users and your website.
